name: agentsmd
description: CODEX-CODING-SPEC — a global coding-discipline spec enforced by native Codex hooks: SPINE workflow, level classification, Iron Laws (no done without fresh evidence), §8 SAFETY (blocks rm -rf $VAR / remote-exec), four-section reports, banned-vocab on commits, and a base-branch CI ship gate — plus a rule-hit telemetry closed loop that lets data decide which rules stay always-on. Installs standalone and is fully independent of oh-my-codex.

interface.shortDescription: A coding-discipline spec enforced by native Codex hooks + a rule-hit telemetry loop
interface.longDescription: agentsmd turns a global coding spec (SPINE workflow, level classification, evidence-before-done Iron Laws, §8 SAFETY, honest four-section reporting) from a document the model may drift from into an enforced system: native Codex hooks block dangerous shell + banned commit vocabulary + red-CI ship pushes, a Stop-time scan checks report structure, and every hit is logged so `agentsmd-rules` can surface which always-on rules earn their place and which are attention dilution. It manages only its own entries in ~/.codex and never touches oh-my-codex or other plugins.
